creator: ache@nagual.pp.ru
text:
On Thu, Nov 30, 2000 at 11:47:12 -0500, mi@aldan.algebra.com wrote:
> On 30 Nov, [Here was data encoded in koi8-r, which can not be displayed] wrote:
> = On Wed, Nov 29, 2000 at 16:54:01 -0500, Mikhail Teterin wrote:
> = > 	If your LANG is set  to ru_SU.KOI8-R or ru_UA.KOI8-U and
> = > 	you see the word  "×ÏÍÂÁÔ" properly, try double clicking
> = > 	on  it  in  an  xterm. Xterm  will  place  "ÏÍÂÁÔ"  into
> = > 	selection, instead  of the whole thing.  For some reason
> = > 	both '×' and '÷' are always treated like that.
> = 
> = This sort of reports must be directed to Xterm maintainer.
> 
> Sorry, I thought, this is may be a locale thing...

As Thomas inform me, currently Xterm not use locale for character classes.

-- 
Andrey A. Chernov
